summary: Patched /Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md and re-reviewed it. No findings rejected.
markdown_response:
Patched `/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md` end to end and kept edits limited to that file.

Formal re-review result: no remaining plan-level contradictions found against the supplied review. I also addressed the delegated spot-review leftovers around bypass test wording, skipped ML-ready output metadata, derived-input freshness, GDELT default handling, official industry manifest reuse/discovery rules, benchmark import coverage, and deprecation sequencing.

Validation performed: re-read the full plan and searched for stale/conflicting terms including `--skip-gdelt-doc-api`, subprocess/deprecation sequencing, stale `20260424` usage, benchmark import coverage, and GDELT default behavior. No code tests were run because this was a markdown-only plan patch.
resolved_findings: Sequenced deprecation after shared callable step APIs and production orchestrator migration, so direct CLI guards cannot break the canonical command.

Split ML-ready boundaries into `feature_snapshot_end`, `entry_data_end`, `label_complete_through`, `ml_training_label_end`, and `prediction_only_signal_end`; specified skipped metadata vs completed training artifacts.

Replaced the broad freshness gate with an endpoint and derived-input freshness registry, including requiredness by mode, lag, retry/backoff, blockers, optional stale handling, and consuming artifacts.
Changed GDELT DOC API handling from default-on/skip-flag to opt-in `--enable-gdelt-doc-api`, with missing/stale DOC diagnostics recorded as optional skipped status by default.
Changed official industry manifest behavior so daily/monthly defaults validate and reuse a pinned manifest, with discovery/build only when coverage/staleness requires it or full-refresh requests it.
Added a central per-CLI bypass helper contract requiring explicit real arguments for production-sensitive defaults and output paths.
Added `quant-import-benchmark-constituents` to the manual-step policy and tests, with explicit handling if documented as an official import.
Tightened deprecation tests so bypass preserves standalone behavior only after required explicit arguments are confirmed.
Added tests for derived-input freshness, skipped ML-ready outputs, default GDELT opt-in behavior, and sequencing of deprecation guards after internal API migration.
